chunks.text: Pravin Varaiya was the Nortel Networks Distinguished Professor in the Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley. From 1975 to 1992 he was also Professor of Economics at Berkeley. From 1994 to 1997 he was Director of the California PATH program, a multi-university research program dedicated to the solution of California's transportation problems. Varaiya held a Guggenheim Fellowship and a Miller Research Professorship. He received Honorary Doctorates from L'Institut National Polytechnique de Toulouse and L'Institut National Polytechnique de Grenoble, and the Field Medal and Bode Lecture Prize of the IEEE Control Systems Society. He was a Fellow of IEEE, a member of the National Academy of Engineering, and a Fellow of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences.
chunks.question: What were Pravin Varaiya's key academic roles and honors?
chunks.answer: Distinguished Professor, Guggenheim Fellow, IEEE Fellow.

chunks.text: He was on the editorial board of 'Discrete Event Dynamical Systems' and 'Transportation Research---C'. He co-authored three books and over 300 technical papers. The second edition of High-Performance Communication Networks (with Jean Walrand and Andrea Goldsmith) was published by Morgan-Kaufmann in 2000. Structure and Interpretation of Signals and Systems (with Edward Lee) was published by Addison-Wesley in 2003. Prof. Varaiya passed away on June 10, 2022.
chunks.question: What editorial roles and publications did Pravin Varaiya have?
chunks.answer: Edited journals, co-authored books and papers.

chunks.text: Education: 1966, Ph.D., Electrical Engineering, University of California, Berkeley. 1960, B.S., Electrical Engineering, University of Bombay.
chunks.question: What degrees did Pravin Varaiya earn?
chunks.answer: Ph.D. from UC Berkeley, B.S. from Bombay.

chunks.text: Research Areas: Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR); Information, Data, Network, and Communication Sciences (IDNCS); Power and Energy (ENE). Control; Networks; Power systems; Transportation.
chunks.question: What were Pravin Varaiya's main research areas?

chunks.text: Research Centers: Berkeley Deep Drive (BDD); Center for the Theoretical Foundations of Learning, Inference, Information, Intelligence, Mathematics and Microeconomics at Berkeley (CLIMB); CITRIS People and Robots (CPAR); Partners for Advanced Transit and Highways (PATH).
chunks.question: Which research centers was Pravin Varaiya associated with?

chunks.text: Selected Publications: M. Malboubi, A. Bahai, M. Ergen, P. Varaiya, and J. Walrand, 'Unequal importance image communication over heterogeneous networks,' in Proc. 65th IEEE Vehicular Technology Conf. (VTC '07-Spring), Piscataway, NJ: IEEE Press, 2007, pp. 629-633. S. Timofeev, A. Bahai, and P. Varaiya, 'Wideband adaptive beamforming system for speech recording,' in Proc. 2007 IEEE Intl. Conf. on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP '07), Vol. 2, Piscataway, NJ: IEEE Press, 2007, pp. 989-992.
chunks.question: What are examples of Pravin Varaiya's selected publications?
chunks.answer: Papers on image communication and beamforming systems.

chunks.text: A. A. Kurzhanskiy and P. Varaiya, 'Ellipsoidal techniques for reachability analysis of discrete-time linear systems,' IEEE Trans. Automatic Control, vol. 52, no. 1, pp. 26-38, Jan. 2007. R. Rajagopal, M. Wainwright, and P. Varaiya, 'Universal quantile estimation with feedback in the communication-constrained setting,' in Proc. 2006 IEEE International Symposium on Information Theory (ISIT 2006), Piscataway, NJ: IEEE Press, 2006, pp. 836-840.
chunks.question: What topics did Pravin Varaiya's publications cover?
chunks.answer: Reachability analysis and quantile estimation techniques.

chunks.text: S. C. Ergen and P. Varaiya, 'PEDAMACS: Power efficient and delay aware medium access protocol for sensor networks,' IEEE Trans. Mobile Computing, vol. 5, no. 7, pp. 920-930, July 2006. R. Jain and P. Varaiya, 'Simulation-based uniform value function estimates of Markov decision processes,' SIAM J. Control and Optimization, vol. 45, no. 5, pp. 1633-1656, June 2006.
chunks.question: What protocols and methods did Pravin Varaiya research?

chunks.text: J. Shu and P. Varaiya, 'Smart pay access control via incentive alignment,' IEEE J. Selected Areas in Communications, vol. 24, no. 5, pp. 1051-1060, May 2006. A. B. Kurzhanski and P. Varaiya, 'Ellipsoidal techniques for reachability under state constraints,' SIAM J. Optimization and Control, vol. 45, no. 4, pp. 1369-1394, April 2006.
chunks.question: What were Pravin Varaiya's contributions to control and communications?
chunks.answer: Smart pay access and ellipsoidal reachability techniques.

chunks.text: Z. Jia, R. Gupta, J. Walrand, and P. Varaiya, 'Bandwidth guaranteed routing for ad-hoc networks with interference consideration,' in Proc. 10th IEEE Symp. on Computers and Communications, Los Alamitos, CA: IEEE Computer Society, 2005, pp. 3-9. M. Ergen and P. Varaiya, 'Throughput formulation and WLAN optimization in mixed data rates for IEEE 802.11 DCF mode,' in Proc.2004 IEEE Global Telecommunications Conf. Workshops (GLOBECOM '04), Piscataway, NJ: IEEE Press, 2004, pp. 266-269.
chunks.question: What networking topics did Pravin Varaiya publish on?
chunks.answer: Ad-hoc routing and WLAN optimization methods.

chunks.text: Selected Honors and Awards: IEEE Simon Ramo Medal, 2022; AACC O. Hugo Schuck Award, 2020; IEEE ITS Lifetime Achievement Award, 2018; International Federation for Automatic Control (IFAC) Fellow, 2017; IEEE ITS Outstanding Research Award, 2009; Richard E. Bellman Control Heritage Award, 2008; Berkeley Citation, 2006; IEEE CSS Hendrik W. Bode Lecture Prize, 2005; IEEE Control Systems Award, 2002; National Academy of Engineering (NAE) Member, 1999; Electrical Engineering Award for Outstanding Teaching, 1998; American Academy of Arts and Sciences Member, 1985; Institute of Electrical & Electronics Engineers (IEEE) Fellow, 1980; Guggenheim Fellow, 1971.
chunks.question: What awards did Pravin Varaiya receive?
chunks.answer: IEEE Simon Ramo Medal, Bellman Award, Guggenheim Fellow.
